items.title: AI inference startup Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B months after its last mega-round
items.raw_content: Startup Baseten is reportedly close to finalizing a $1.5 billion round at a $13 billion as the “inference gold rush" marches on.
items.summary: Startup Baseten is reportedly close to finalizing a $1.5 billion round at a $13 billion as the “inference gold rush" marches on.

items.title: OpenAI is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its IPO
items.raw_content: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.
items.summary: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.

items.title: Amazon hopes to challenge Nvidia more directly by selling its AI chips
items.raw_content: AWS is in talks to sell its chips to other data centers. CEO Andy Jassy has said this represents a $50 billion opportunity for the company.
items.summary: AWS is in talks to sell its chips to other data centers. CEO Andy Jassy has said this represents a $50 billion opportunity for the company.

items.title: The film about Sam Altman has been dropped by Amazon MGM
items.raw_content: Luca Guadagnino's film about OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, Artificial, has reportedly been dropped by Amazon MGM. The film, which stars Andrew Garfield and covers the rollercoaster five days in 2023 spanning Altman's termination and reinstatement as CEO, had been in the works for about a year. The cast also includes A Complete Unknown actress Monica […]

items.title: Barret Zoph is out at OpenAI again after just five months
items.raw_content: Five months after returning to OpenAI, Barret Zoph - the company's head of enterprise AI sales - has departed, The Verge has learned. Zoph returned to OpenAI in mid-January after a stint as co-founder and CTO of Thinking Machines Lab, the competing AI company founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati. Shortly after Zoph returned […]

items.title: The CEO of Allbirds’ new AI biz has a plan, but no employees
items.raw_content: Call it a startup with a sole founder and a very large seed round, but what's next is less clear.
items.summary: Call it a startup with a sole founder and a very large seed round, but what's next is less clear.

items.title: The US says ASML’s top chip tool may be in China. ASML says it isn’t
items.raw_content: There's a commercial logic that cuts against the idea that ASML would risk its export license to arm a Chinese customer.
items.summary: There's a commercial logic that cuts against the idea that ASML would risk its export license to arm a Chinese customer.

items.title: Source: Elastic agrees to buy CRV-backed DeductiveAI for up to $85M
items.raw_content: DeductiveAI, a startup that uses AI to catch and resolve bugs in software, was founded just three years ago.
items.summary: DeductiveAI, a startup that uses AI to catch and resolve bugs in software, was founded just three years ago.

items.title: Snap spins off AI video team into new company, Dotmo, due to costs
items.raw_content: The Snapchat maker is spinning off yet another internal unit. Dotmo will be composed of current Snap staff who are leaving the social media company to focus on AI video development.
items.summary: The Snapchat maker is spinning off yet another internal unit. Dotmo will be composed of current Snap staff who are leaving the social media company to focus on AI video development.

items.title: Almost half of US singles feel negatively about AI in dating, Match says
items.raw_content: About 47% of singles look negatively at the use of AI in dating -- but many dating app users are open to AI helping with profile punch-ups and conversation starters.
items.summary: About 47% of singles look negatively at the use of AI in dating -- but many dating app users are open to AI helping with profile punch-ups and conversation starters.

items.title: AI data centers just got a government-mandated fast lane to the grid
items.raw_content: FERC told grid operators to give data centers a fast lane for interconnections, but it failed to address electricity supply shortages.
items.summary: FERC told grid operators to give data centers a fast lane for interconnections, but it failed to address electricity supply shortages.

items.title: Amazon employees say they’re facing termination for backing data center limits
items.raw_content: When three Amazon software engineers testified earlier this month at Seattle City Council hearings about data centers, they started their testimony by citing a city law barring employment discrimination over political speech. Now, they're accusing their employer of breaking that law by retaliating against them. On June 10th - one week after the hearing, and […]

items.title: The smartphone era created an attention crisis — slow tech is fixing it
items.raw_content: “People just really want to take back control of their time, their lives, their attention... They’re down for whatever helps them do that.”
items.summary: “People just really want to take back control of their time, their lives, their attention... They’re down for whatever helps them do that.”

items.title: Adobe’s redesigned AI studio remembers what your creations look like
items.raw_content: Adobe is introducing some new capabilities for its Firefly AI assistant, alongside a "reimagined" AI studio that lets you edit and generate new designs from a single interface. The new Firefly experience launching today in private beta is designed to give you "persistent context, reusable assets, and organized workflows" across your projects, according to Adobe, […]

items.title: Photoshop and Premiere now have AI assistants
items.raw_content: Adobe's plan to stick AI assistants into all of its Creative Cloud suite is now fully underway, with new chatbots now rolling out to its biggest editing and design apps. items.title: Two-thirds of Americans think AI is advancing too quickly
items.raw_content: According to the latest Pew Research poll, 49 percent of Americans report using chatbots at least occasionally, but 63 percent think the tech is advancing too quickly. Overall, use of AI chatbots has increased dramatically since 2024, when only 33 percent reported using them. Specifically, ChatGPT's usage has doubled since 2023, with 44 percent of […]
